notes.note: A. Trebonius was a Roman financier with substantial business affairs (negotia) in Cilicia, the province Lentulus Spinther governed as proconsul. This is a letter of recommendation (commendatio) asking the governor's favour for him.

notes.anchor: his freedmen, his agents, his household
notes.note: A Roman businessman abroad operated through his liberti (freedmen), procuratores (agents managing his affairs), and familia (slave staff). Cicero commends the whole apparatus of Trebonius's provincial operation to the governor's protection.

notes.anchor: what T. Ampius decreed about his case
notes.note: T. Ampius Balbus, a previous proconsul (of Asia, c. 58 BC), had issued a ruling in Trebonius's favour; Cicero asks Lentulus to uphold his predecessor's decree, the continuity of edicts being a normal courtesy between governors.
